title: Package bundled example samples correctly
description: Fix the installation/package issue where the bundled resource/examples folder ends up empty for some users. Verify the assets are shipped and discoverable on supported platforms.

title: Investigate Cubase macOS plugin detection
description: Investigate reports that Cubase on macOS does not recognize the RVRSE VST3. Verify whether quarantine removal, install location, or a Cubase-specific validation issue is the root cause and capture the required remediation.

title: Investigate Studio One / Fender Studio Pro plugin detection
description: Investigate why RVRSE is not recognized in Studio One 8 and Fender Studio Pro despite VST3 support. Determine whether the issue is scan-path configuration, validation, quarantine/signing, or a host-specific compatibility problem, and document the fix path.

title: Clarify macOS install path and xattr instructions
description: Fix the documentation/install guidance bug where macOS users confuse ~/Library and /Library for VST3 installation, causing the xattr command to fail on the wrong path. Add explicit system-vs-user examples and quarantine-removal troubleshooting.

title: Post-Release Epic — BUGS
description: Top-priority post-v1.0.0 bug collection epic. Holds install, compatibility, sample-loading, packaging, and host-specific defects raised by release feedback.

title: macOS: ad-hoc codesign all bundles in CI
description: Downloaded macOS zip triggers Gatekeeper 'damaged' error because binaries are unsigned. Add codesign --force --deep -s - for all four bundles (VST3, AU, CLAP, standalone) in both publish-develop.yml and release.yml. Update INSTALL-macOS.txt with xattr -cr instructions.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #20 — ad-hoc codesign after staging, install instructions updated

title: VST3: MIDI CC does not update GUI knobs
description: SendParameterValueFromAPI() pushes to mParamChangeFromProcessor queue, but in IPlugAPIBase::OnTimer() that queue is only consumed in the non-VST3 (#else) branch. For VST3, SendParameterValueFromDelegate is a NOOP in the processor. Fix: use a custom IPlugQueue<ParamTuple> pushed from the audio thread CC handler, drained in OnIdle() which runs on the UI thread for all formats, calling SendParameterValueFromDelegate().
close_reason: Fixed in PR #19 — custom IPlugQueue drains in OnIdle() for all formats

title: Fix MIDI CC not updating stutter Rate/Depth knobs
description: MIDI CC1 (Rate) and CC11 (Depth) correctly update internal param values via GetParam()->Set() at RVRSE.cpp:865-868, but: (1) The UI knobs don't move because SendParameterValueFromAPI() is not called to queue the change for the GUI. (2) stutterRateHz/stutterDepth are cached at ProcessBlock() lines 725-726 before MIDI processing, so CC changes don't take effect until the next audio buffer. Fix: call SendParameterValueFromAPI() after Set(), and move stutter param reads inside the per-sample loop (after MIDI queue processing).
close_reason: Fixed: SendParameterValueFromAPI + cached stutter locals (PR #18)

title: Fix Windows VST3/CLAP resource embedding (main.rc not linked)
description: VST3 and CLAP plugin targets on Windows don't embed Win32 resources (fonts, images) because iPlug2's CMake only adds main.rc to the APP target. Workaround: manually add main.rc with RC include paths to VST3/CLAP targets in RVRSE/CMakeLists.txt.
close_reason: Fixed: added main.rc to VST3/CLAP targets on Windows. PR #17 merged.

title: Fix Windows resource embedding (fonts + logo missing from main.rc)
description: main.rc only embedded Roboto-Regular.ttf. Missing Roboto-Bold.ttf and logo.png cause assertion failure and crash on Windows when opening the UI.
close_reason: Fixed: added Roboto-Bold.ttf and logo.png to main.rc for Windows resource embedding. PR #16 merged.

title: Reverb tail truncation — output buffer same size as input, no tail generated
close_reason: Fixed: source buffers now padded with 5s silence before reverb, trimmed after. Build + tests pass.

title: Unit tests for existing DSP modules
description: Write Catch2 unit tests for all existing offline DSP: Reverb.h (applyReverb, applyReverbStereo), BufferUtils.h (reverseBuffer, resampleLinear, applyTailFadeOut), TimeStretch.h (stretchBuffer, calcStretchFactor), SampleLoader (LoadSample). Tests should verify correctness with known inputs/golden outputs. Depends on rverse-6fl (Catch2 setup).
close_reason: 42 unit tests implemented and passing across all DSP modules: Constants, BufferUtils, Reverb, TimeStretch, Stutter, SampleLoader

title: Add Catch2 test framework and tests/ directory
description: Add Catch2 via CMake FetchContent. Create tests/ directory with CMakeLists.txt. Add a placeholder test that compiles and runs. Wire into cmake --build build --target test. This is the foundation for DSP unit tests in Phase 2.
close_reason: Catch2 v3.7.1 wired via FetchContent. 6 smoke tests passing. CTest integrated.

title: Implement real-time stutter gate (Stutter.h)
description: Per-sample rhythmic gate. stutterPeriod = samplesPerBeat / rateSubdivision. Gate computed as square wave with 50% duty cycle. Depth controls wet/dry mix of gate. Audio thread only — never called from offline pipeline. Must be MIDI CC responsive with zero latency (changes take effect on the next sample).
close_reason: Stutter.h: per-sample rhythmic gate with EStutterRate enum (Off to 1/2 note), StutterState, stutterProcess(), tempo-synced. Wired into ProcessBlock riser path. Builds clean on all 4 formats.

title: Implement riser + hit playback (RvrseVoice)
description: Real-time voice that plays final_riser[] from the start, fires the dry hit at the calculated beat offset (Riser Length * samplesPerBeat), and applies a fade envelope. Reads from pre-computed buffers only — no allocations on the audio thread. MIDI note-on starts riser, both voices release naturally.
close_reason: Riser + hit dual-voice playback working. Riser plays on note-on, dry hit fires at beat offset. Sample rate resampling for 96kHz+ files. BPM sync via GetTempo(). Tail fade-out (kRiserTailFadeBeats). Note-off re-trigger bug fixed. Tested in DAW.

title: Implement OLA time-stretcher
description: Overlap-Add time-stretcher as a stateless function. stretchFactor = (riserLengthBeats * samplesPerBeat) / reversed_buffer.size(). Recalculate final_riser[] on Riser Length or BPM change. Quality is less critical than correctness for MVP. Offline pipeline only.
close_reason: OLA time-stretcher in TimeStretch.h: Hann-windowed overlap-add with 50% overlap, normalised output. calcStretchFactor() helper maps beats+BPM to stretch ratio. kOlaWindowSize=2048 in Constants.h. Compiles clean.

title: Implement buffer reversal
description: In-place reverse of the lushed (reverbed) buffer. Simple std::reverse or manual swap. Part of the offline pipeline — never called from audio thread.
close_reason: In-place stereo buffer reversal in BufferUtils.h — std::reverse on deinterleaved L/R vectors. Offline pipeline only. Compiles clean.

title: Implement Schroeder algorithmic reverb
description: Implement a Schroeder/Moorer-style algorithmic reverb as a stateless function: applyReverb(in, out, numSamples, lushAmount). Parallel comb filters feeding series allpass filters. Lush knob maps to room size + wet gain. No external libraries. Must run in offline pipeline only (never on audio thread).
close_reason: Schroeder/Moorer reverb implemented in Reverb.h: 8 parallel combs + 4 series allpasses with LP damping. Stereo version uses independent engines per channel. Lush knob maps to room size + wet gain. All constants in Constants.h. Compiles clean, 0 warnings.

title: Write initial README.md
description: Create a concise but complete README covering: project description, build prerequisites, build instructions (macOS + Windows), plugin formats produced, and basic usage. Keep it lean — update incrementally as features land.
close_reason: README.md created at project root with: project description, architecture overview (two-layer split, file map, data flow diagrams), sample loading flow, offline pipeline stages, real-time playback loop, build instructions (macOS + Windows), plugin formats, DAW usage, project structure. RVRSE/README.md updated to point to root.

title: Implement MIDI note-on sample playback
description: Simplest possible playback: MIDI note-on triggers the loaded hit sample. No riser, no effects. Just load a sample, press a MIDI key, hear the hit. This validates the full signal path (MIDI in → sample read → audio out) and provides a testable foundation for all subsequent features. Include a rudimentary GUI with a Load Sample button and basic plugin info.
close_reason: MIDI note-on triggers sample playback. Velocity scaling, sample-accurate timing, 5ms anti-click fade-out on note-off. Tested in standalone app — works cleanly.

title: Implement sample loading with dr_libs
description: Integrate dr_libs (header-only). Load WAV and AIFF files into a float32 stereo buffer. Provide a file-open mechanism (minimal GUI button or drag-drop). Store source_buffer[] and hit_buffer[] for later use by the offline pipeline and real-time voice.
close_reason: Sample loading implemented with dr_libs. WAV+AIFF via dr_wav.h. Load button + file dialog + background thread loading. All 4 formats build clean.

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI (Windows + macOS)
description: Create GitHub Actions workflows to auto-build VST3, AU, and CLAP artefacts on every push. Separate workflows for Windows and macOS. Verify CI passes with the empty plugin scaffold.
close_reason: GitHub Actions CI live — macOS + Windows, builds + 42 tests, Node.js 24 actions

title: Set up iPlug2OOS repo and CMake build
description: Clone iPlug2OOS template, scaffold the RVRSE plugin project, configure CMakeLists.txt, create Constants.h and CHANGELOG.md. Verify empty plugin builds on macOS. Establish the src/ and resources/ directory structure per RVRSE_BRIEF.md.
close_reason: iPlug2OOS scaffold complete. All 4 formats (VST3, AU, CLAP, APP) build and deploy on macOS/arm64. CMake+Ninja pipeline working.

title: Set up C++ development environment (macOS)
description: Install Xcode command-line tools, CMake, and verify the C++17 compiler toolchain. Ensure all prerequisites for iPlug2 development are in place.
close_reason: C++ environment verified: Apple Clang 17.0 (C++17, arm64), CMake 4.2.3, Ninja 1.13.2, pkg-config installed. All tools confirmed working via test builds.

title: Add Linux build and distribution support
description: Ship a Linux build of RVRSE as part of the post-v1.0.0 roadmap. Cover build, packaging, and basic validation for the supported Linux plugin targets.

title: Export processed audio
description: Add a focused export workflow for the processed result in v1.1.0. Keep the scope limited to exporting audio cleanly, without bundling in broader drag-out or render-in-place workflows unless they naturally fit the implementation.

title: Add play button for non-MIDI preview
description: Add a dedicated play/preview button so users can audition the current processed result without setting up MIDI first. Keep this separate from export and other workflow features.

title: Add drag-and-drop sample import
description: Allow users to drag a sample directly onto the plugin to load it instead of relying only on a file browser. Keep the scope focused on drag-in loading only.

title: Surface unsupported compressed sample formats clearly
description: When users attempt to load compressed formats that are not yet supported, detect that case cleanly and show a clearer error instead of a generic PCM read failure. Support for MP3 and similar formats remains out of scope until v1.2.0.

title: Adaptive riser overlap for high stretch ratios
description: At high stretch ratios, the time-stretcher smears out the reversed transient tail, creating a long volume drop-off before the hit. The fixed 1/32-beat overlap is insufficient. Fix: make overlapBeats proportional to the stretch factor: overlapBeats = min(kRiserOverlapBeatsBase * max(1.0, stretchFactor), kRiserOverlapBeatsMax). Also scale the tail fade to cover the full overlap region.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #19 — adaptive overlap scales with stretch factor

title: Smooth riser-to-hit transition with crossfade overlap
description: The transition from the reversed riser to the hit sample is too abrupt. Currently the hit fires exactly when the riser buffer ends (mHitOffset = riser->NumFrames()), and a 1/16-beat tail fade (kRiserTailFadeBeats) creates a gap of silence before the hit. Fix: introduce a configurable overlap (kRiserHitOverlapBeats) so the hit fires slightly before the riser ends, creating a natural crossfade. The riser's existing tail fade will blend with the hit's attack for a seamless transition.
close_reason: Fixed: 1/32-beat overlap with beat-aligned hit offset (PR #18)

title: Add debug playback mode — expose intermediate pipeline buffers
close_reason: Debug Stage parameter implemented with 4 modes (Normal/Reverbed/Reversed/Riser Only). Intermediate buffers stored in RiserData, cached for stretch-only rebuilds. UAT Scenario 5 added.

title: Upgrade OLA time-stretcher to phase vocoder or WSOLA
notes:
**Transient smearing at riser end (2026-04-05):**
User confirmed via Debug Stage that the OLA stretcher smears the steep reversed transient at the riser's tail (the original sample's attack, reversed). This is most audible when stretching a short sample to long riser lengths (e.g. 16 beats).

Potential approach: non-uniform stretch — leave the transient region near the riser's end unstretched (or minimally stretched) and apply more stretching toward the tail/beginning of the riser where the reverb wash is smooth and tolerant of phase artifacts. This preserves the punch of the hit transition while still achieving the target riser length.

Also consider upgrading from basic OLA to WSOLA or phase vocoder for better overall quality.
close_reason: PR #8 merged: signalsmith-stretch integration with quality param, offline sync, all review fixes applied

title: Expose remaining DSP parameters (Lush, Riser Length, Fade In, Hit Volume, Dry/Wet)
description:
Several parameters are coded in the offline pipeline but not exposed as IParams:
- Lush (reverb wet amount, 0-100%) — Reverb.h ready, needs kParamLush
- Riser Length (0.25-16 beats) — RvrseProcessor.h supports it, needs kParamRiserLength
- Fade In (0-100%) — Constants.h has default, needs kParamFadeIn
- Hit Volume (-inf to +6 dB) — playback coded, needs kParamHitVolume
- Dry/Wet (0-100%) — not yet coded
Add to EParams, init in constructor, wire in ProcessBlock. These are testable via DAW generic editor even before GUI lands.
close_reason: PR #5 squash-merged into develop. All 8 DAW params + debug stage exposed and tested.

title: Replace installer license.rtf placeholder with actual MIT license
description: RVRSE/installer/license.rtf contains: 'THIS IS A PLACEHOLDER LICENCE PROVIDED WITH IPLUG2 — IT HAS NO LEGAL BASIS'. Replace with the actual MIT license text matching the root LICENSE file. Must be done before any public release or installer distribution.
close_reason: PR #7 merged. license.rtf now contains actual MIT license.

title: Release Preparation Epic — v1.0.0
description:
Umbrella epic for all work required before the v1.0.0 public release of RVRSE.

Phases:
1. Critical Fixes — Branding, license, CI setup
2. Feature Completion — GUI, pitch shift, state persistence, expose all params
3. Documentation & Polish — User manual, CONTRIBUTING, CODE_OF_CONDUCT, version sync
4. QA & Release — Platform validation, pluginval, tag + publish

See child issues for full breakdown. A task is release-blocking if it is P0 or P1.
close_reason: v1.0.0 released — user manual deferred to v1.1.0

title: Persist loaded sample path across sessions (state save/restore)
description:
When a DAW saves a project, RVRSE must persist the file path of the currently loaded sample so it is automatically reloaded when the project is reopened. Implementation needs:
1. Override SerializeState() / UnserializeState() in iPlug2 to save/restore the sample file path.
2. On restore: check if the file still exists. If yes, reload it into the offline pipeline.
3. If the file is missing or unreadable: display a user-visible warning (e.g. via SendMsgFromUI or a status text in the GUI), and leave the plugin in an empty/ready state without crashing.
4. Handle edge cases: path encoding (UTF-8), cross-platform path separators, very long paths.
5. Update CHANGELOG.md and README.md when done.
close_reason: PR #6 squash-merged. Sample path persists across DAW sessions with graceful missing-file handling.

title: Add git hooks and Context7 agent instructions
description: Add versioned git hooks (commit-msg, pre-commit, pre-push) to enforce beads IDs in commits, catch merge markers/large files/secrets, and block direct pushes to main. Add Context7 MCP documentation to AGENTS.md. Update section numbering.
close_reason: Git hooks (commit-msg, pre-commit, pre-push) added in hooks/ dir. Context7 section added to AGENTS.md. Section numbering updated. README updated with hooks setup step.

title: Build full IGraphics GUI (dark theme)
description: Build the complete GUI per RVRSE_BRIEF.md layout: three zones (riser panel, hit panel, bottom bar). Dark themed. IKnobControl for all parameters with labels. MIDI CC indicator (◉) on stutter knobs. Load Sample button. BPM display. Master Volume + Dry/Wet knobs in bottom bar.
notes:
UI Design Workflow Decision: We will follow a UX → UI → Implementation pipeline.

1. UX Brief — Define user flows, param groupings, interaction patterns
2. UI Design — External AI agent specialised in UI design will produce mockups/specs, informed by iPlug2 IGraphics capabilities (native controls only — no web UI)
3. Implementation — Build in IGraphics based on approved designs

Constraint: NO web-based UI (WebView/WASM). Use iPlug2 native IGraphics framework (NanoVG/Metal on macOS, NanoVG/GL2 on Windows). This keeps the plugin lightweight.

The external design agent will need a capability brief covering:
- Available IGraphics controls (IKnobControl, IVSliderControl, ITextControl, ISVGControl, etc.)
- Colour/theming system (IVStyle, IColor)
- Layout model (IRECT-based, no CSS/flexbox)
- Custom drawing capabilities (IControl::Draw with NanoVG)
- Bitmap vs vector tradeoffs
- Animation support (IAnimationFunction)
close_reason: GUI complete: 9 steps implemented — zones, header, footer, riser panel, hit panel, waveform display, hit preview, MIDI indicator, resize polish. All docs updated.

title: Implement MIDI CC for Stutter Rate + Depth
description: Wire MIDI CC1 (mod wheel) to Stutter Rate and CC11 (expression) to Stutter Depth. Hardcoded defaults per Constants.h. CC changes update parameters instantly (per-sample). Future: user-assignable CC (out of scope for MVP).
close_reason: MIDI CC1 (mod wheel) → Stutter Rate, CC11 (expression) → Stutter Depth. Uses ControlChange() normalised API. Both params also exposed as IParam for DAW automation.

title: Wire offline-to-real-time buffer handoff
description: Lock-free transfer of final_riser[] from RvrseProcessor (background thread) to RvrseVoice (audio thread). Use an atomic flag or double-buffer swap. No allocations or locks on the audio thread.
close_reason: RvrseProcessor wired into RVRSE class. Sample loads trigger offline pipeline. ProcessBlock polls isNewRiserReady() for lock-free riser consumption. OnReset propagates sample rate. mRiserBuffer available for audio-thread playback.

title: Implement offline pipeline orchestrator (RvrseProcessor)
description: Chains: load → reverb → reverse → stretch. Runs on a background thread, never on audio thread. On offline parameter change, rebuild from the appropriate stage onwards (e.g. Lush change re-runs everything; Riser Length change only re-stretches). Writes result into final_riser[].
close_reason: RvrseProcessor.h: full offline pipeline orchestrator. Chains reverb→reverse→stretch on background thread. Generation-based abort for stale builds. Caches reversed buffer to skip reverb on stretch-only changes. Lock-free output handoff via atomic shared_ptr. Compiles clean.

title: Add support for compressed audio formats
description: Add support for common compressed sample formats such as MP3 and similar codecs. Update the documentation and error handling so supported and unsupported formats are always explicit.

title: Add crossfade controls between reverse tail and hit
description: Add dedicated crossfade shaping for the seam between the reversed tail and the dry hit. Keep this separate from trimming and region selection work.

title: Add sample region selection
description: Allow users to select an arbitrary start/end region within a longer source file so only the chosen content is processed. Keep this separate from simple cut-in trimming.

title: Post-Release Epic — v1.2.0
description: Follow-up feature epic for larger sample-editing and format-expansion work that should stay out of the tighter v1.1.0 scope.

title: Restructure release roadmap from collected feedback
description: Reorganize the post-release Beads roadmap based on consolidated user feedback. Create a dedicated BUGS epic, keep v1.1.0 features small and tidy, split region selection/crossfade/compressed formats into later work, and preserve documentation as part of v1.1.0 without committing the temporary feedback file.
close_reason: Roadmap updated from consolidated release feedback

title: BPM display not showing in Windows Cubase VST3
description: BPM text control stays at 'BPM: —' in Cubase VST3 on Windows. Likely cause: Cubase may not populate processContext->tempo until transport is playing, and GetTempo() returns 0.0. The guard 'if (hostBPM > 0.0)' prevents the update. Fix options: (1) check kTempoValid flag in processContext.state, (2) fall back to kDefaultBPM when host hasn't provided tempo yet, (3) show 'BPM: —' but update as soon as transport starts. See RVRSE.cpp:749-757 (ProcessBlock BPM read) and RVRSE.cpp:455-469 (OnIdle BPM display update). Milestone: v1.1.0.

title: Publish CI artifacts to GitHub Releases
description:
Add artifact publishing to CI workflow:

1. **Develop branch** (rolling pre-release): On each push to develop, build Debug, package per-platform zips (VST3/AU/CLAP), and publish to a rolling 'develop-latest' GitHub pre-release (force-updated each time). Version string: 0.1.0-dev.

2. **Tagged releases** (production): On v* tag push, build Release mode, package per-platform zips, run Inno Setup for Windows installer, and create a proper GitHub Release with all artifacts attached.

Packaging:
- macOS: zip containing .vst3, .component, .clap
- Windows: zip containing .vst3, .clap + Inno Setup .exe installer
- Include install instructions in README

Parent: rverse-0v6 (Release Preparation Epic)
close_reason: Merged PR #12 — publish-develop.yml + release.yml + INSTALL files

title: Cut-in trimming (sample start trim)
description:
Allow users to set a non-destructive start offset into the loaded hit sample so dead air or soft buildup before the transient can be trimmed away.

Scope for v1.1.0 only:
- Draggable cut-in marker on the waveform preview
- Everything before the marker is visually dimmed and excluded from playback and offline riser generation
- Offset persists with plugin state
- Keep this feature strictly about sample start trimming

Out of scope:
- Arbitrary start/end region selection
- Crossfade shaping between reversed tail and dry hit

close_reason: Debug Stage param, enum, intermediate buffers all wrapped in #ifndef NDEBUG. Both Debug and Release configs build clean. 43 tests pass.

title: Improve reverb quality — reduce Schroeder comb coloration

Plist files and RVRSE.iss still show version 0.0.0 while config.h has 0.1.0. Either:
1. Add a script (e.g. scripts/bump-version.py) that reads config.h PLUG_VERSION_STR and updates all plists + RVRSE.iss, OR
2. Wire the existing iPlug2 update_version scripts into the CMake build so versions propagate automatically.

description:
The LaTeX skeleton at RVRSE/manual/RVRSE manual.tex is empty. Write a proper user manual covering:
- Installation (macOS AU/VST3/CLAP, Windows VST3/CLAP, standalone)

Generate PDF from LaTeX. Include in GitHub release artefacts.

title: Replace all AcmeInc placeholder branding with SamuFL identity
description:
The iPlug2 project generator left AcmeInc placeholders throughout the codebase. Replace with actual SamuFL branding. Known locations (non-exhaustive):
- config.h (PLUG_MFR, PLUG_URL_STR, PLUG_EMAIL_STR, etc.)
- installer/*.rtf (mailto links, displayed emails, company name)
- Info.plist templates in resources/ (NSHumanReadableCopyright, etc.)
- scripts/makezip-win.py, makedist-win.bat (any AcmeInc references)

Owner will provide full company details (legal name, URL, email, copyright line) before work begins. Subsumes the narrow scope of rverse-rar (installer RTFs).

description: Download pluginval and run it against RVRSE.vst3, RVRSE.clap, and RVRSE.component in the GitHub Actions CI workflow. Pluginval catches crashes, threading bugs, and parameter issues. Should be a hard gate — CI fails if pluginval fails.
close_reason: Pluginval CI added for VST3 (macOS+Windows) and AU (macOS). Pinned v1.0.4 with SHA256. Also fixed stale AU plist metadata (aufx->aumu, 9c0G->5SpI). PR #14 merged.

title: Tag v1.0.0 and publish GitHub release

close_reason: v1.0.0 tagged and pushed — release.yml triggered

title: Validate on macOS (Cubase + Studio One + Logic)
description: Manual QA on macOS: load sample, trigger via MIDI, tweak Stutter CC, verify timing accuracy, test all parameter ranges. Test in Cubase, Studio One, and Logic. Verify VST3, AU, and CLAP formats.
close_reason: UAT passed on macOS — Cubase VST3 validated by user

title: Validate on Windows (Cubase + Studio One)
description: Manual QA on Windows: load sample, trigger via MIDI, tweak Stutter CC, verify timing accuracy, test all parameter ranges. Test in both Cubase and Studio One. Verify VST3 and CLAP formats.
close_reason: UAT passed on Windows — Cubase VST3 validated by user

title: Implement WaveformView control
description: IControl subclass showing the complete riser→hit sequence as one continuous waveform. Reversed/washed riser on the left, sharp hit transient on the right. Playhead scrubs during playback for live visual feedback. Updates when final_riser[] is rebuilt.
close_reason: Completed as part of GUI epic (rverse-ebv). WaveformControl + HitPreviewControl implemented in WaveformControl.h.

title: Implement Riser Tune + Hit Tune (pitch shift)
description: Linear resampling for pitch shifting. Riser Tune (-24 to +24 st) applied during riser playback. Hit Tune (-24 to +24 st) applied during hit playback. Simple playback rate change — no FFT or granular synthesis needed for MVP. Riser Tune responds to CC2.

title: Add MIDI CC mapping visibility and MIDI learn
description: Improve CC mapping clarity and support MIDI learn / reassignment for performance controls. This is a low-priority v1.1.0 feature and can be cut if higher-priority bugs and workflow improvements consume the release.

title: Upstream PR: fix iPlug2 CMake resource embedding for Windows plugin targets
description:
iPlug2's IPlugPlugin.cmake (Scripts/cmake/IPlugPlugin.cmake) only adds main.rc to the APP (standalone executable) target on Windows (lines 94-105 of _iplug_create_desktop_targets). The VST3, CLAP, VST2, and AAX MODULE targets (lines 127-155) do NOT include main.rc, so no fonts, images, or other Win32 resources get embedded into plugin DLLs.

This means on Windows, plugin formats (VST3, CLAP, etc.) cannot load fonts or bitmaps declared in main.rc — only the standalone .exe works. This is a bug in iPlug2's CMake build system.

FIX: In _iplug_create_desktop_targets(), for each plugin format block (VST3, CLAP, VST2, AAX), add the same main.rc handling that the APP block has:
1. Check if WIN32 and main.rc exists
2. Append main.rc to the target's sources
3. Set RC compiler include flags: /I resources/fonts /I resources/img /I resources

The APP block (lines 94-105) is the reference implementation. Replicate the rc_file logic for all add_library(MODULE) targets in the same function.

TARGET REPO: https://github.com/iPlug2/iPlug2 — open a PR against the master branch with the fix and a clear explanation of the bug.

title: Add Dependabot / dependency version monitoring
description: Enable Dependabot for GitHub Actions version updates. Consider a scheduled CI job or Dependabot-like check for pluginval and iPlug2 submodule freshness. Low priority — current deps are pinned and stable.

title: Post-Release Epic — v1.1.0
description: Small, user-facing v1.1.0 feature epic. Keep issues narrow and shippable: cut-in trimming, drag-in loading, play preview, audio export, Linux, documentation, and low-priority CC mapping / MIDI learn.

title: Move setLush/setRiserLength calls off the audio thread
Add CONTRIBUTING.md and CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
description:
Create standard open-source community files:
- CONTRIBUTING.md: How to report bugs, submit PRs, coding standards, beads workflow, git-flow branching
- CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md: Contributor Covenant or similar
These are expected by the community for any public GitHub repo.
close_reason: CONTRIBUTING.md + CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md added in release/1.0.0

title: Set up platform installers (InnoSetup for Windows, pkg for macOS)
description:
Create proper installers for end-user distribution:
- Windows: InnoSetup installer (scaffold already exists in RVRSE/installer/). Copies VST3 + CLAP to Program Files, optional standalone app.
- macOS: .pkg installer that copies AU to ~/Library/Audio/Plug-Ins/Components/, VST3 + CLAP to their respective dirs.
- Both should show license, readme, and allow component selection.
- Depends on rverse-g67 (branding) being done first so installers show correct company info.
For initial releases, a zip with install instructions is sufficient. This is for polish before wider public release.
close_reason: Won't do — distributing via GitHub Releases zips, no platform installers

title: Fix placeholder content in Windows installer RTF files
description:
Address PR review comment on installer/readme-win.rtf:
- mailto:: has double colon (should be mailto:)
- Displayed email acmeinc@SamuFL.com doesn't match the mailto target support@acmeinc.com
- All installer RTF files contain iPlug2 template placeholders (AcmeInc references)
- Replace with actual RVRSE project contact info when available.
close_reason: Won't do — no longer using Windows installer

title: Clean up Windows packaging scripts (makedist-win.bat, makezip-win.py)
description:
Address PR review comments on iPlug2 scaffold scripts:
1. makedist-win.bat: FOR /F captures all stdout from makezip-win.py, overwriting ZIP_NAME. Fix to capture only the final zip name.
2. makezip-win.py: Debug output interferes with batch capture. Add quiet/machine-output mode, redirect logs to stderr.
3. makezip-win.py: Use raw strings or os.path.join for Windows backslash paths to avoid escape warnings.
These are iPlug2 template files — fix when we actually set up Windows CI/packaging.
close_reason: Won't do — no longer using Windows installer/packaging scripts

title: Add tempo-synced stutter rate option
description: Add a toggle or mode switch to Stutter.h allowing the stutter rate to be tempo-synced (musical subdivisions: 1/32 to 1/2 note) in addition to the current continuous Hz mode. When tempo-synced, the period should be calculated from host BPM and the selected subdivision. The UI should expose a mode selector (Continuous / Synced). Low priority — continuous mode is the default for creative flexibility.
close_reason: Won't do — not needed for v1.0.0 or v1.1.0

title: Add headless integration tests (ProcessBlock)
description: Create integration tests that instantiate the RVRSE plugin class directly (no GUI, no DAW), feed it MIDI events and silence buffers, and verify audio output. Includes: load a known WAV, trigger riser, compare output against golden-master reference. Uses Catch2 as the test runner.

title: TMP inspect json shape
description: Temporary issue to inspect bd create JSON shape; will be closed immediately.
close_reason: Temporary inspection issue
